key_insight: Stabilization learning reframes machine learning around stability and feedback, unifying control theory and learning for adaptive behavior under perturbations.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical framework for robust control, observation, and recognition in high-dimensional nonlinear systems where reliability matters more than pure optimality.
method: Deep feature extraction with data-driven feedback synthesis
Unified six-tuple framework expanded to seven-tuple models for constrained learning and tracking
evidence_signal: The abstract supports the framework by formally reformulating 11 representative problems across control, robotics, games, and multi-agent tasks.
watch_item: The framework is broad and abstract, so real deployments may face nontrivial choices in defining state spaces, metrics, policies, and stability constraints.
paper_title: Stabilization Learning: A Paradigm Transition Bridging Control Theory and Machine Learning
